summary: The reproduction starts a minimal Mercure hub with anonymous subscriptions and the active-subscriptions API enabled, opens a subscription, and then retrieves the active subscription list without sending any JWT.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated request to `/.well-known/mercure/subscriptions` should return HTTP 200 and expose the active subscriber UUID and topic selector even though no subscriber JWT was provided.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned `https://github.com/dunglas/mercure.git` at commit `13c9ef40001cac5026f1c9eca2fa2e36f706168a`, built the reproduction container, opened an anonymous subscription on `https://example.com/private-feed/user-42`, and then received HTTP 200 plus that topic and its subscriber UUID from `/.well-known/mercure/subscriptions` without a JWT. The run printed `[evohunt] PROOF: anonymous subscription metadata exposed without a JWT` and exited successfully.
verification_method: The container-side verifier runs a Go harness against the pinned checkout and requires two concrete signals: the exact proof string `[evohunt] PROOF: anonymous subscription metadata exposed without a JWT` and the leaked topic line `"topic": "https://example.com/private-feed/user-42"` in the unauthenticated JSON response.
evidence_produced_by_run: The verifier emitted `[evohunt] PROOF: anonymous subscription metadata exposed without a JWT` before the host script printed the success banner.
limitations_or_blockers: The session checkout does not include Git metadata, so the rerunnable bundle pins the upstream Mercure repository commit `13c9ef40001cac5026f1c9eca2fa2e36f706168a`, where the same vulnerable logic was revalidated during this run.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
